_comment: TEMPLATE / SOURCE-OF-TRUTH for the APIFY REQUEST QUEUE ('frontier') layer — the deduplicated, lockable, resumable URL frontier the self-audit crawl pulls from. This is the ONE managed-storage primitive the rest of the integrations did not yet wire (ingest/standby/webhooks/schedules/exports/proxy/notify/mcp are already covered). It holds NO credentials; queueId/actorIds are PLACEHOLDERS and deployed:false. The live queueId + APIFY_TOKEN are supplied by the operator at the LAST deploy step. Nothing here performs a network call or enqueues a real request. See docs/apify/frontier.md.

queue._note: A NAMED request queue (named queues persist; unnamed default queues are wiped between runs). One queue per audited subject keeps each subject's frontier isolated — no cross-subject bleed. The name carries a non-reversible subject_token, never a raw label/url (Closure-Mode / minimum-disclosure).

_dedup_doctrine: uniqueKey is the deduplication key Apify enforces server-side: a second addRequest with a uniqueKey already in the queue returns the EXISTING request and adds nothing (wasAlreadyPresent:true). We make uniqueKey the canonicalized URL so the SAME public surface is fetched AT MOST ONCE per audit. Dedup is not a perf trick here, it is COMPLIANCE: minimum-disclosure / anti-dragnet — never re-pull a page we already hold.
_scope_note: Enqueue is the SAME chokepoint as ingest: every URL is routed through shared/scope.js validateScope() (read-only require) before it may enter the frontier, and any URL whose host is in PRIVATE_SOCIAL_HOSTS is REFUSED entry. A queue can never widen scope; it inherits the gate's verdict.

caps._note: Hard ceilings. max_enqueue_batch=25 mirrors the Apify batch-add API limit (POST /v2/request-queues/{queueId}/requests/batch accepts at most 25 requests per call). max_queue_size is the anti-dragnet floor: once the frontier holds this many distinct surfaces, further enqueues are REFUSED (clamped to zero), never grown. A self-audit reads a bounded own-surface, it does not crawl the open web.

lock._note: head-and-lock (GET /v2/request-queues/{queueId}/head/lock?lockSecs=) hands the head request to ONE client exclusively for lockSecs, so two parallel crawl clients never fetch the same surface at once. This is politeness, not evasion: it SLOWS concurrent pressure on a target host. lockSecs is clamped DOWN to max_lock_secs, never up.

request_defaults._note: userData rides with each request through the queue. respectRobotsTxtFile is forced true and CANNOT be turned off by the caller (anti-evasion floor, same as ingest.config forced_overrides). scope_reasserted records that validateScope() passed at enqueue time so a downstream consumer can re-check fail-closed.

forced_overrides._why: Robots compliance is not negotiable. Even if a caller passes respectRobotsTxtFile:false in userData, the builder overwrites it to true before the request may be enqueued. Mirrors integrations/ingest/ingest.config.json and the proxy policy.

blocked_host_note: Host-level blocking (login-walled private social) is NOT re-implemented here — it lives in shared/scope.js (PRIVATE_SOCIAL_HOSTS) and is enforced by reading validateScope() before any request is built. Two doors: validateScope AND this frontier policy.
